perturbation.original_text: 1.2 It is hereby agreed that none of the Undersigneds shall, directly, or indirectly, for itself or themselves, or through or on behalf of any other person or entity including, without limitation, family members, trusts, or other business or estate planning arrangements engage in the following: (a) at any time, divulge, transmit, or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted, or otherwise disclose, any business contacts, client, distributor, or customer lists, technology, know-how, traded secrets, marketing techniques, supplier contacts, contracts, or other confidential or proprietary information of the Corporation of whatever nature existing on or prior to the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information shall not be considered to be confidential or proprietary if (i) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (ii) it is generally known in the industry in which the Business is engaged, or (iii) the Undersigneds can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement); (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for any reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America or any other country in which Purchaser sells products, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and (c) at any time during the Restrictive Period initiate contact with any employee, consultant, or independent contractor of Purchaser for the purpose of hiring away such employee, consultant, or independent contractor from Purchaser, or solicit customers of the Purchasers.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 It is hereby agreed that none of the Undersigneds shall, directly, or indirectly, for itself or themselves, or through or on behalf of any other person or entity including, without limitation, family members, trusts, or other business or estate planning arrangements engage in the following: (a) at any time, divulge, transmit, or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted, or otherwise disclose, any business contacts, client, distributor, or customer lists, technology, know-how, traded secrets, marketing techniques, supplier contacts, contracts, or other confidential or proprietary information of the Corporation of whatever nature existing on or prior to the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information *shall* be considered to be confidential or proprietary even if (i) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (ii) it is generally known in the industry in which the Business is engaged, or (iii) the Undersigneds can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement); (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for any reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America or any other country in which Purchaser sells products, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and (c) at any time during the Restrictive Period initiate contact with any employee, consultant, or independent contractor of Purchaser for the purpose of hiring away such employee, consultant, or independent contractor from Purchaser, or solicit customers of the Purchasers.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that information is *not* considered confidential if it is common knowledge, publicly recorded, or previously known to the recipient. The modified text changes "shall not be considered" to "*shall* be considered", creating an in-text contradiction. This contradicts the exceptions provided in the original clause by making even publicly available or commonly known information confidential. This creates uncertainty around what information the Undersigneds are restricted from disclosing under section 1.2(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 It is hereby agreed that none of the Undersigneds shall, directly, or indirectly, for itself or themselves, or through or on behalf of any other person or entity including, without limitation, family members, trusts, or other business or estate planning arrangements engage in the following: (a) at any time, divulge, transmit, or otherwise disclose or cause to be divulged, transmitted, or otherwise disclose, any business contacts, client, distributor, or customer lists, technology, know-how, traded secrets, marketing techniques, supplier contacts, contracts, or other confidential or proprietary information of the Corporation of whatever nature existing on or prior to the date hereof (provided, however, that for purposes hereof, information *shall* be considered to be confidential or proprietary even if (i) it is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (ii) it is generally known in the industry in which the Business is engaged, or (iii) the Undersigneds can demonstrate that such information was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement); (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for any reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America or any other country in which Purchaser sells products, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and (c) at any time during the Restrictive Period initiate contact with any employee, consultant, or independent contractor of Purchaser for the purpose of hiring away such employee, consultant, or independent contractor from Purchaser, or solicit customers of the Purchasers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 1.2(a) to state that information *shall* be considered confidential even if it is common knowledge, publicly recorded, or previously known. This directly contradicts the original intention of the clause, which stated that such information *shall not* be considered confidential. Because the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, there is a direct contradiction between the definition of confidential information in the same clause, making it impossible to determine what the Undersigneds are restricted from disclosing.

perturbation.original_text: 1.  RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS. --------------------- 1.1 The Undersigneds acknowledge and agree that: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing and distribution techniques, and other aspects of the business of the Corporation have been of value to the Corporation, and have provided the Corporation (and will hereafter provide the Purchaser) with substantial competitive advantage in the operation of its business, and (ii) by virtue of their previous relationships with the Corporation as officers, managers, Shareholders, employees, and/or affiliates, the Undersigneds have detailed knowledge and possess confidential information concerning the business and operations of the Corporation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.  RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS. --------------------- 1.1 The Undersigneds acknowledge and agree that: (i) the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing and distribution techniques, and other aspects of the business of the Corporation have *no* value to the Corporation, and have provided the Corporation (and will hereafter provide the Purchaser) with substantial competitive advantage in the operation of its business, and (ii) by virtue of their previous relationships with the Corporation as officers, managers, Shareholders, employees, and/or affiliates, the Undersigneds have detailed knowledge and possess confidential information concerning the business and operations of the Corporation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "have been of value" to "have *no* value" in section 1.1, the contract introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 1.1(i) now states that the Corporation's business assets are *not* valuable, while Section 1.1(ii), and the entire premise of the non-compete agreement, implies that this confidential information and business knowledge are valuable, and worth protecting. The introductory paragraph F. also states that the Undersigneds have agreed to enter into this agreement to *induce* the purchaser. This contradiction raises the question of whether there is a genuine, valid reason for the restrictive covenants, making the agreement vulnerable to challenge, given the contradiction with section F.
perturbation.contradicted_text: F. In order to induce the Purchaser to consummate the transactions contemplated by the Purchase Agreement, the Undersigneds have agreed and the Purchaser has required the Undersigneds to enter into this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, know-how, trade secrets, marketing and distribution techniques, and other aspects of the business of the Corporation have *no* value to the Corporation in section 1.1(i). This directly contradicts section F, which states that the Undersigneds have agreed to enter into the agreement to *induce* the Purchaser, implying that their business knowledge and confidential information *do* have value to the Corporation and are worth protecting. This creates a clear inconsistency in the document and fulfills the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition as the value assessment of business assets contradicts the justification for the agreement itself.

perturbation.original_text: (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for any reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America or any other country in which Purchaser sells products, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and (c) at any time during the Restrictive Period initiate contact with any employee, consultant, or independent contractor of Purchaser for the purpose of hiring away such employee, consultant, or independent contractor from Purchaser, or solicit customers of the Purchasers.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for *no* reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America or any other country in which Purchaser sells products, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and (c) at any time during the Restrictive Period initiate contact with any employee, consultant, or independent contractor of Purchaser for the purpose of hiring away such employee, consultant, or independent contractor from Purchaser, or solicit customers of the Purchasers.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "for any reason" to "for *no* reason" in the definition of the 'Restrictive Period' under section 1.2(b) introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text limits the restrictive period to two years after termination *for any reason*. The modified text implies the restrictive period applies to situations where there is *no* termination. This makes the duration of the restrictive period unclear, potentially subjecting the Undersigneds to indefinite restrictions, depending on how "no reason" is interpreted. This creates conflict within section 1.2 itself, particularly regarding the applicability and duration of the restrictive covenants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) at any time during the period of the Purchase Agreement and for two years following the termination of the Purchase Agreement for *no* reason (the "Restrictive Period"), invest, carry on, engage, or become involved, either as an employee, agent, advisor, officer, director, stockholder (excluding ownership of not more than 3% of the outstanding shares of a publicly held Corporation if such ownership does not involve managerial or operational responsibility), manager, partner, joint venture, participant, or consultant, in any business enterprise (other than the Purchaser or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, or assigns) which (i) is located or operating, or soliciting customers located in the United States of America or any other country in which Purchaser sells products, and (ii) is or becomes, at any time during the Restrictive Period, engaged in the manufacture, assembly, sale, marketing, advertising, and distribution of golf balls or any golf related products. For purposes of this Agreement, golf related products shall mean products that are marketed or sold by Purchaser; and (c) at any time during the Restrictive Period initiate contact with any employee, consultant, or independent contractor of Purchaser for the purpose of hiring away such employee, consultant, or independent contractor from Purchaser, or solicit customers of the Purchasers.
perturbation.justification: The change from "for any reason" to "for no reason" introduces a direct conflict regarding the conditions under which the restrictive period applies. The original text specifies the restrictive period begins after termination of the Purchase Agreement 'for any reason'. The modified text suggests the restrictive period applies even when there is 'no' termination, contradicting the original condition for its commencement. This directly affects the interpretation and enforceability of the clause and the contradicted text is also the same text as the Changed Text, making it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 In the event of a breach of this Agreement by any either of the Undersigneds, the precise amount of damages that may be suffered by the Purchaser of reason of such breach may be difficult to ascertain; accordingly, the parties hereby agree that, as liquidated damages (and not as a penalty) in respect of any such breach, the Purchaser, in its sole discretion, may choose to demand the sum of $20,000 as the sole and exclusive damages for the breach. The parties agree that the foregoing provision for liquidated damages, if chosen by Purchaser, constitutes a fair and reasonable estimate of the actual damages that might be suffered by reason of a breach of this Agreement by the 2 <PAGE> Undersigneds. Alternatively, and in lieu of its right to demand liquidated damages as aforesaid, the Purchaser may elect to pursue and recover all actual damages, incurred by the Purchaser as a result of such breach, insofar as they can be determined.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 In the event of a breach of this Agreement by any either of the Undersigneds, the precise amount of damages that may be suffered by the Purchaser of reason of such breach may be difficult to ascertain; accordingly, the parties hereby agree that, as liquidated damages (and not as a penalty) in respect of any such breach, the Purchaser *must* demand the sum of $20,000 as the sole and exclusive damages for the breach. The parties agree that the foregoing provision for liquidated damages, if chosen by Purchaser, constitutes a fair and reasonable estimate of the actual damages that might be suffered by reason of a breach of this Agreement by the 2 <PAGE> Undersigneds. Alternatively, and in lieu of its right to demand liquidated damages as aforesaid, the Purchaser may elect to pursue and recover all actual damages, incurred by the Purchaser as a result of such breach, insofar as they can be determined.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 4.1, the clause "may choose to demand" is changed to "*must* demand". This directly contradicts the sentence immediately following it which states "Alternatively, and in lieu of its right to demand liquidated damages as aforesaid, the Purchaser *may elect* to pursue and recover all actual damages". One sentence mandates liquidated damages, while the next allows the Purchaser to pursue actual damages. This creates uncertainty regarding the remedies available to the Purchaser, and directly contradicting itself
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 In the event of a breach of this Agreement by any either of the Undersigneds, the precise amount of damages that may be suffered by the Purchaser of reason of such breach may be difficult to ascertain; accordingly, the parties hereby agree that, as liquidated damages (and not as a penalty) in respect of any such breach, the Purchaser *must* demand the sum of $20,000 as the sole and exclusive damages for the breach. The parties agree that the foregoing provision for liquidated damages, if chosen by Purchaser, constitutes a fair and reasonable estimate of the actual damages that might be suffered by reason of a breach of this Agreement by the 2 <PAGE> Undersigneds. Alternatively, and in lieu of its right to demand liquidated damages as aforesaid, the Purchaser may elect to pursue and recover all actual damages, incurred by the Purchaser as a result of such breach, insofar as they can be determined.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 4.1, from 'may choose to demand' to 'must demand', directly contradicts the subsequent sentence that provides the Purchaser the alternative option to 'elect to pursue and recover all actual damages'. The modified text makes liquidated damages mandatory, while the following sentence still suggests it's an optional alternative. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as to whether the purchaser can choose between liquidated damages or actual damages, and is using Structural Flaws as a method of contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Assignment. The benefits and obligations of any party to this ---------- Agreement may not be assigned, except upon the written consent of the other party. This Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall be enforceable by and inure to the benefit of, the parties named herein and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Assignment. The benefits and obligations of any party to this ---------- Agreement *may* be assigned *without* the written consent of the other party. This Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall be enforceable by and inure to the benefit of, the parties named herein and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 5.2 states that the benefits and obligations *may not* be assigned without written consent. The changed text states that the benefits and obligations *may* be assigned *without* written consent. The rest of the paragraph still states that the agreement is binding on successors and assigns, which normally implies the possibility of assignment. The modified clause directly contradicts the initial statement regarding the requirement for written consent, thus creating uncertainty and confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Assignment. The benefits and obligations of any party to this ---------- Agreement *may* be assigned *without* the written consent of the other party. This Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall be enforceable by and inure to the benefit of, the parties named herein and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that assignment requires written consent, while the changed text allows assignment without written consent. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, re-emphasizing the allowance of assignment without consent. Since they are directly opposed statements regarding the need for written consent for assignment, this constitutes a strong, direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.9 Dispute Resolution. Any controversy, claim, or dispute among the ------------------ parties hereto arising out of or related to this Agreement or the breach hereto, which cannot be settled amicably by the parties, shall be submitted for mediation in Salt Lake City, Utah. In the event mediation is unsuccessful, the parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of an appropriate court within Salt Lake County, State of Utah, to hear and decide any controversy, claim, or dispute hereunder. The prevailing party in any legal action shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, as determined by the trial court.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.9 Dispute Resolution. Any controversy, claim, or dispute among the ------------------ parties hereto arising out of or related to this Agreement or the breach hereto, which cannot be settled amicably by the parties, *will not* be submitted for mediation in Salt Lake City, Utah. In the event mediation is unsuccessful, the parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of an appropriate court within Salt Lake County, State of Utah, to hear and decide any controversy, claim, or dispute hereunder. The prevailing party in any legal action shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, as determined by the trial court.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall be submitted for mediation" to "*will not* be submitted for mediation", the text now negates the initial commitment to mediation as the first step in dispute resolution. Given that the next sentence speaks of mediation being unsuccessful and then heading to litigation, this leads to an in-text contradiction and contradiction with the natural order of the agreement, throwing the enforceability of the dispute resolution process as a whole into question.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.9 Dispute Resolution. Any controversy, claim, or dispute among the ------------------ parties hereto arising out of or related to this Agreement or the breach hereto, which cannot be settled amicably by the parties, *will not* be submitted for mediation in Salt Lake City, Utah. In the event mediation is unsuccessful, the parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of an appropriate court within Salt Lake County, State of Utah, to hear and decide any controversy, claim, or dispute hereunder. The prevailing party in any legal action shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, as determined by the trial court.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that disputes "shall be submitted for mediation." The changed text modifies this to state that disputes "*will not* be submitted for mediation." This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the same section, as the text both requires and prohibits mediation. This change directly conflicts with the requirement for mediation implied within the original agreement and creates a structural flaw as it results in directly contradictory statements within the same section.
